title: Explore the Wonders of India with Ease
contentHtml:
India, a land of ancient traditions and modern marvels, offers an experience like no other. From the snow-capped peaks of the Himalayas to the sun-kissed beaches of Goa, the bustling streets of Mumbai to the serene backwaters of Kerala, India invites travelers on a journey through diverse landscapes, vibrant cultures, and rich heritage.

At DU Global, we understand that exploring India should be as seamless as possible. That’s why we offer a hassle-free eVisa service, designed to make your entry into this incredible country both smooth and efficient. Our streamlined process ensures that you can focus on planning your adventure while we take care of the paperwork.

title: Take the First Step to Company Setup in the UAE
contentHtml:
Setting up a company in the UAE has become one of the most attractive options for global entrepreneurs, startups, and investors. With recent reforms allowing 100% foreign ownership in most sectors, tax-friendly regulations, and world-class infrastructure, the UAE continues to be a preferred business hub connecting Asia, Europe, and Africa.

Whether you are looking at the Dubai mainland or a UAE free zone, choosing the right jurisdiction depends on factors such as location, industry, office space requirements, share capital rules, and compliance. That’s where DU Digital Global comes in — guiding you through the entire process with efficiency and expertise, ensuring your setup is smooth and stress-free.

title: Company Formation in the Mainland
contentHtml:
Establishing a company in Dubai offers businesses unparalleled access to both local and international markets, under the regulation of the Dubai Department of Economic Development (DED). With recent legislative changes allowing 100% foreign ownership across numerous sectors, entrepreneurs can enjoy complete control over their ventures. Dubai mainland companies benefit from the flexibility to operate in a wide range of industries, engage in government contracts, and expand their presence throughout the UAE.

Setting up a mainland company in Dubai provides businesses with direct access to the UAE’s local market as well as international trade opportunities. Under the regulation of the Dubai Department of Economic Development (DED), mainland companies enjoy:

100% foreign ownership in most activities (after the 2021 reforms)
Flexibility to engage in a wide range of industries
Eligibility to bid for government contracts
Freedom to expand across all Emirates
For entrepreneurs seeking scalability and long-term stability, mainland company formation remains one of the strongest options.

title: Company Formation in Freezone
contentHtml:
UAE freezone company setup offers businesses a range of advantages tailored to foster growth and innovation. Free Zones in Dubai are designed to attract foreign investment by providing benefits like 100% foreign ownership, complete repatriation of profits, and exemption from corporate and personal taxes. These zones cater to specific industries, offering a supportive environment with industry-focused infrastructure and services.

UAE free zones are designed to attract foreign investors by providing business-friendly ecosystems and benefits like:

100% foreign ownership
Full repatriation of capital and profits
0% personal income tax
Customs duty exemptions within the free zone
Industry-specific infrastructure (IT, logistics, finance, media, etc.)
With over 40 free zones across the UAE (including Dubai’s IFZA, Meydan, RAKEZ, and SPC), each caters to different industries and offers flexible office space solutions — from flexi-desks to full-fledged headquarters.

title: Tailored Business Solutions
contentHtml: Get in touch with us to take advantage of our specialized packages and clever company setup in the UAE.

title: Outstanding Affinity
contentHtml: With our seamless help, starting a business in the UAE won't take long to be up and running.

title: Massive Diversity
contentHtml: When establishing a business in the UAE, there is a vast array of industries, sectors, and commercial endeavors to select from.

sectionKey: Benefits of Establishing a Business in the UAE
title: Benefits of Establishing a Business in the UAE
contentHtml:
100% Foreign Ownership – Full control of your company in free zones and most mainland sectors.
Tax Advantages – No personal income tax and a competitive 9% corporate tax (only on profits above AED 375,000).
Profit Repatriation – Freedom to transfer 100% of profits and capital abroad.
Global Market Access – Strategic location connecting Asia, Europe, and Africa.
Ease of Doing Business – Fast setup, world-class infrastructure, and investor-friendly policies.

sectionKey: Types of Business License in the UAE
title: Commercial License
contentHtml: Tailored for businesses involved in trading activities, this license permits importing, exporting, distributing, and storing goods within the UAE. Whether dealing in electronics, textiles, or luxury items, a commercial permit forms the cornerstone for retail and wholesale businesses.

sectionKey: Types of Business License in the UAE
title: Tourism License
contentHtml: In a nation famed for its hospitality and tourism sector, the tourism license serves as an entry point for businesses in travel agencies, tour operators, and hospitality services. With Dubai's ambitious tourism aspirations, this license presents ample prospects for HNIs to capitalize on the flourishing tourism industry.

sectionKey: Types of Business License in the UAE
title: Industrial License
contentHtml: A company set up in Dubai which is involved in manufacturing, processing, assembling, and packaging activities requires an industrial license. This license allows companies to set up manufacturing facilities within the Free Zone. Industrial license enables the establishment of industrial enterprises, factories, and production facilities.

sectionKey: Entity Options for Free Zone Company Setup in the UAE
title: Entity Options for Free Zone Company Setup in the UAE
contentHtml:
A company setup in the UAE of a free zone firm is easy and simple, thanks to the regulators who encourage foreign investors with low capital needs. When starting a company in these zones, Indian entrepreneurs can pick from three main types of entities:

Branch of a foreign or local company
Free Zone Establishment (FZE) with a single shareholder
Free Zone Company (FZC) with 2 to 5 shareholdersCompany (FZC) with 2 to 5 shareholders

sectionKey: Steps to Set Up a Business in the UAE Free Zones
title: Choose Legal Entity
contentHtml: Decide on the type of entity you want to establish in the free zone, considering the capital requirement for that particular zone.

sectionKey: Steps to Set Up a Business in the UAE Free Zones
title: Select a Trade Name
contentHtml: Pick your company’s unique and permissible trade name, ensuring it is not already registered

sectionKey: Steps to Set Up a Business in the UAE Free Zones
title: Apply For a Business License
contentHtml: Submit the required documents and apply for the appropriate business license based on your company’s primary activity.

sectionKey: What is a Serbia D-Type Visa?
title: What is a Serbia D-Type Visa?
contentHtml: A Serbia D-Type (Long Stay) Visa is designed for foreign nationals who have secured a job in Serbia and plan to stay longer than 180 days. It is the required pathway for those wishing to work and reside legally in Serbia for up to 1 year, with the possibility of extension.

title: Who Can Apply?
contentHtml: Nationals from India, Nepal, Bangladesh and Sri Lanka — Served by the Serbia Embassy in New Delhi—are eligible to apply.

title: Required Document Checklist
contentHtml:
Document	Who Provides
Scanned copy of original and valid passport	Applicant
Education Certificate	Applicant
Recent passport-size photograph (white background)	Applicant
Official Invitation Letter	Employer
Certificate of Company Registration	Employer
Signed Employment Contract	Employer
Job Description / Role Summary	Employer

title: Single Entry VISA
contentHtml: The Malaysia Single Entry Visa is a type of visa that allows individuals to enter Malaysia once during the visa validity period for a specific purpose, such as tourism, business, or visiting family or friends. Once the visa holder leaves Malaysia, the visa becomes invalidated, and a new visa is required for re-entry.

sectionKey: What is a Greece National Visa (Type D) for Employment?
title: What is a Greece National Visa (Type D) for Employment?
contentHtml: The Greece National Visa (Type D) is issued to non‑EU/EEA nationals who have secured employment in Greece and intend to stay longer than 90 days. Once you arrive, you must apply for a Residence Permit with the Right to Work, under Greek law (Law 4251/2014). This visa is the entry route for long‑term employment in the hospitality and support sectors.

sectionKey: What Should Indians Know Before Visiting Japan?
title: What Should Indians Know Before Visiting Japan?
contentHtml:
Japan, situated as an island nation in East Asia, lies along the eastern shoreline of the Asian continent, spanning from the Sea of Okhotsk in the north to the East China Sea and the Philippine Sea in the south. Renowned as the 'Land of the Rising Sun', Japan boasts 20 UNESCO World Heritage sites.

Beyond these, the country is celebrated for its natural landscapes, intricate gardens, ancient temples, vibrant festivals, diverse cuisine, significant historical landmarks, and captivating theme parks. Given this rich history, it's no wonder that Japan draws millions of tourists annually.

If you want to visit the country and witness the beauty, securing a tourist visa is essential. In this guide, we delve into the necessary steps for an Indian national to obtain a Japan tourist visa, also referred to as a short-term visa.

sectionKey: Essential Travel Tips for Indian Tourists Visiting Japan
title: Essential Travel Tips for Indian Tourists Visiting Japan
contentHtml:
Punctuality:
Be prepared for Japan’s renowned efficiency. Familiarize yourself with the extensive network of trains, buses, and subways. Consider purchasing a JR Pass for convenient travel between major cities to make your travel smooth

Cash vs. Cards:
While credit cards are gaining acceptance, carrying cash is still recommended for smaller purchases and local eateries. ATMs are widely available in major cities and tourist areas.

Respect the culture:
Be mindful of Japanese customs. Learn about bowing, a gesture of respect. It’s customary to take off shoes indoors and avoid loud noises in public areas like trains and libraries. Tipping is generally not expected, a simple “ありがとうございした (Arigatou Gozaimashita)” – meaning “Thank you very much” – suffices.

Language & communication:
While English proficiency is improving, basic Japanese phrases will go a long way. Downloading translation apps or carrying a Japanese phrasebook can be helpful for everyday interactions.

sectionKey: Top Tourist Destinations in Japan for Indian Travelers
title: Top Tourist Destinations in Japan for Indian Travelers
contentHtml:
Step into the world of your favourite anime! Visit real-life locations that inspired iconic scenes. try your hand at making traditional dishes like Takoyaki, Hike the majestic Mount Fuji, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, and witness the ethereal beauty of the Japanese Alps.

Tokyo:
The vibrant capital city with immense energy. Explore iconic landmarks like Tokyo tower, shopping districts, and futuristic neighbourhoods.

Kyoto:
The cultural heart of Japan. Visit ancient temples, serene gardens, and witness the Geisha district of Gion.

Osaka:
Experience the lively food scene and the renowned Dotombori district.

title: Must-Visit Destinations for Indian Travelers
contentHtml:
Hiroshima Peace Memorial Park:
Pay your respects at this poignant reminder of the devastating effects of war and a symbol of hope for peace.

Japanese Alps:
Trek through the picturesque mountains, dotted with charming villages and offering stunning natural beauty.

Okinawa:
Discover the tropical paradise south of mainland Japan. Relax on pristine beaches, explore vibrant coral reefs, and experience the unique Ryukyu culture.

sectionKey: Japan Tourist Visa Fees, Validity & Processing Time for Indian Citizens
title: Japan Tourist Visa Fees, Validity & Processing Time for Indian Citizens
contentHtml:
30/60 Days Tourist Visa
Processing time: 10 working days after appointment date
Stay period: 30 days
Entry: Single/Multiple
Fees: INR 4,200/-

sectionKey: What Should Indians Know Before Visiting Egypt?
title: What Should Indians Know Before Visiting Egypt?
contentHtml:
Egypt is a country in northeast Africa known for its ancient pyramids, like the Great Pyramid of Giza and the Sphinx. The capital is Cairo, and the Nile River runs through the country, providing water and fertile land. Egypt relies on tourism, agriculture, and the Suez Canal for its economy. Whether you love tourist-friendly environments, the diverse culture, or and its Pyramids, the Republic of Egypt has it all.

Egypt sits between both Asia and Africa, connecting both continents together through the Sinai Peninsula. This amazing country is home to the civilizations of the Pharaohs, Nubians, Persians, Greeks and many more. It is home to the longest and oldest river, i.e. the Nile.

If you are going for a holiday, the best time to experience the beauty of Egypt is from mid-October to May. If you are planning to visit this beautiful country, here are some tips and tricks you should follow to make your travel smooth.

sectionKey: Egypt Visa Application Centres for Indians Now in:
title: Egypt Visa Application Centres for Indians Now in:
contentHtml:
Delhi
Mumbai
Kolkata
Chennai
Silliguri
Bangalore

sectionKey: Essential Travel Tips for Indian Tourists Visiting Egypt
title: Essential Travel Tips for Indian Tourists Visiting Egypt
contentHtml:
The official language of Egypt is Arabic. Although English is widely understood by the population, it is recommended to learn a few important phrases & words in Arabic. 


During summers, Egypt can reach up to 45’C so make sure you carry a lot of sun screen to protect your skin from the sun and a water bottle to hydrate yourself.


During winter nights, temperatures may drop low enough to give you chills, so remember to pack your warm socks and sweaters.


India is only 3.5 hours ahead of Egypt’s time Jetlag in Egypt will never be a problem and won’t disrupt your holidays, so you can go there without any worries.

sectionKey: Top Tourist Destinations in Egypt for Indian Travelers
title: Top Tourist Destinations in Egypt for Indian Travelers
contentHtml:
Famous for its iconic landmarks, the Great Pyramid of Giza, one of the Seven Wonders of the World, remains the oldest and most enduring attraction in Egypt. 

If you hadn’t visited Khan-El-Khalili market, a shopping utopia for enthusiasts, your trip to Cairo, the capital of Egypt, would not be complete. There are many of souks and bazaars to visit, so there are lots of souvenirs to take home.

Your trip wouldn’t be finished without exploring the different deserts and the Valley of the Kings, where nearly 63 ancient royal tombs, including King Tut’s, are found. When visiting Egypt, make sure to see all the important historical sites and immerse yourself in their rich traditions.

sectionKey: Egyptian Cuisine: A Taste Guide for Indian Travelers
title: Egyptian Cuisine: A Taste Guide for Indian Travelers
contentHtml:
Egyptian cuisine is a delicious blend of flavours from different cultures. Their main foods include kebabs, rice, baba ghanoush, and koftas seasoned with their special spices. You’ll taste influences from Greek, Syrian, Turkish, Lebanese, and Palestinian cuisines in their dishes. 

If you’re near the coast, expect more fish-based meals, while further south, you’ll find spicier dishes with North African flavours. Don’t miss out on trying their tasty specialties.

sectionKey: Are You Planning to Visit Egypt?

contentHtml: Experience the wonders of Egypt hassle-free with our reliable visa service tailored for Indian tourists. Whether you’re marveling at the pyramids or savouring Egyptian delicacies, let us take care of your visa needs so you can make the most of your Egyptian adventure. title: What is DuVerify
contentHtml:
DUVerify is an advanced and comprehensive verification platform that is designed to help applicants for visas quickly and accurately verify their identity, nationality, passport, travel history, banking and financial history, income tax data and criminal record.

Our system is capable of conducting detailed checks and producing digital imprints of applicants to ensure their eligibility for the visa application process. Our software is simple and easy to use, and allows applicants to verify themselves in a matter of minutes with 100% accuracy.

DUVerify saves both time and money for applicants, allowing them to quickly and easily verify their credentials without having to visit a consulate or an embassy. DuVerify is the perfect solution for applicants who need to apply for visas, whether it is for a visit, work, business, student or permanent immigration. With our verification platform, applicants can rest assured that their personal information and documents are verified accurately and quickly, making the visa application process a breeze!

title: bottomhero
contentHtml: DU Global Ltd has partnered with Embassy of Georgia in India to provide secure and efficient backend verification of the applicants in the two-step process. With the e-visa system, Indian citizens can apply for their visas online, pay their visa fee, and receive their visas via email within just a few days. To learn more about the new e-visa system or to apply for a visa, please visit the official website of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Georgia – https://www.evisa.gov.ge/GeoVisa/

sectionKey: Revised process to apply Georgia Visa-
title: Revised process to apply Georgia Visa-
contentHtml:
The launch of its new e-visa system, designed to make applying for a visa to Georgia simpler and faster than ever before. Once the application is submitted, applicants will be required to pay the visa fee and upload documents supporting the application.

After successful visa payment applicant will be directed to DUVerify platform to upload more documents and information to verify and strengthen visa application.

After the documents have been verified, applicants will receive their visa via email. The e-visa system is an easy and convenient way for Indian citizens to get their visas quickly and securely. We hope that this new system will make it easier for Indian citizens to visit and explore Georgia. To learn more about the new e-visa system or to apply for a visa, please visit the official website of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Georgia – https://www.evisa.gov.ge/GeoVisa/
